list.label: MySQL (metadata)
list.name: mysql_metadata
list.description: Grafana MySQL (or compatible) datasource that holds **cluster registry** rows. Not part of NCCL — wire to your own DB or change the cluster/region variables to another source.

list.name: cluster
list.description: SQL must return one column `cluster` whose values **equal** the Prometheus cluster label used in your environment (e.g. `cluster`, `s_cluster`). Replace table `slurm_clusters` with your schema.table.
list.label: Cluster (metadata SQL)

list.definition:
SELECT
  telemetry_region
FROM
  slurm_clusters
WHERE
  cluster = ${cluster}
list.name: telemetry_region
list.query:
SELECT
  telemetry_region
FROM
  slurm_clusters
WHERE
  cluster = ${cluster}
list.description: SQL must return `telemetry_region` for the chosen cluster; values should **match a substring** of your Grafana Prometheus/Loki datasource names (see `prom_datasource` / `loki_datasource` regex).
list.label: Telemetry region (metadata SQL)

list.name: prom_datasource
list.description: Prometheus/Mimir instance for this region. Regex `/${telemetry_region}/` filters datasources by name; adjust regex or datasource naming if imports fail.

list.name: loki_datasource
list.description: Optional Loki instance for the same region (regex on name). Panels here are Prometheus-only; safe to ignore if unused.

Replace `your_job_metric` with any Prometheus metric that carries job ids in your environment. Replace `your_cluster_label` and `your_job_label` with the label names used in that metric. Panels filter NCCL metrics on `slurm_job_id` — align `your_job_label` with that label or edit the panel queries.
list.name: jobid
device
list.description: Replace `your_hostname_label` with the Prometheus label that holds the node hostname in your environment (e.g. `Hostname`, `node`, `net_host_name`). Optional; used for downstream hostname filters.

list.definition: query_result(  max by (instance_id) (    count_over_time(      your_node_info_metric{        cluster="$cluster",        hostname=~"(?i).*(${device:regex}).*"      }[$__range]    ) > 0  ))
list.name: csp_instance_id

query.query: query_result(  max by (instance_id) (    count_over_time(      your_node_info_metric{        cluster="$cluster",        hostname=~"(?i).*(${device:regex}).*"      }[$__range]    ) > 0  ))

list.description: Optional; replace `your_node_info_metric` with a Prometheus metric in your environment that exposes per-node instance/cloud IDs, filtering by `cluster` and `hostname`. Remove this variable entirely if not needed.

title: NCCL Inspector Job Performance
description:
**Template — customize datasources and variables before use.**

**Panels require** Prometheus series from NCCL Inspector-style exports, for example:
- `nccl_p2p_bus_bandwidth_gbs`, `nccl_p2p_exec_time_microseconds` (labels such as `cluster`, `slurm_job_id`, `p2p_operation`, `n_nodes`, `message_size`, `nranks`)
- `nccl_bus_bandwidth_gbs`, `nccl_collective_exec_time_microseconds` (labels such as `collective`, `n_nodes`, …)

**Variables** use placeholder metric names (`your_job_metric`, `your_node_info_metric`) and label names (`your_cluster_label`, `your_job_label`, `your_hostname_label`) — replace these with whatever metrics and labels carry this information in your Prometheus environment. Any metric that exposes job ids and hostnames will work; there is no dependency on any specific exporter.

**Cluster / region:** default queries assume a **SQL metadata** table you provide (see variable descriptions). Replace with your schema or switch variables to Prometheus `label_values` if you have no DB.
